claim: Podencos are multi-sensory hunters that use sight, scent, and hearing simultaneously — not purely sighthounds — making them distinct from Galgos which rely primarily on vision.
stance_target: Podencos use multiple sensory channels when hunting/tracking, meaning they will both scent-track and visually pursue when lost
scope_conditions: All Podenco subtypes (Andaluz, Canario, Ibicenco, Maneto, etc.)
quoted_support: Multi-sensory specialised hunters, they have large, highly-mobile pricked ears, keen eyesight and an acute sense of smell.

claim: Podencos can jump over 6-foot fences from a standing position, meaning standard residential fencing is insufficient to contain a prey-driven Podenco.
stance_target: Lost Podencos will cover large distances and can exit seemingly secured areas
scope_conditions: All Podenco subtypes; particularly relevant in Algarve rural/semi-rural terrain
quoted_support: They are agile to the point they have been known to climb trees in pursuit of prey and can even jump great heights from a stand. A secure garden with 6' fence is typically required for this breed.

claim: Podencos are bred with 'endless endurance for trotting rather than galloping', meaning a lost Podenco can sustain movement over large areas for extended periods unlike sprint-specialized sighthounds.
stance_target: Lost Podencos have wide movement radii due to high endurance, not just burst speed
quoted_support: All have been bred with a combination of speed and agility with endless endurance for trotting rather than galloping.

claim: Hunting dogs including Podencos were explicitly excluded from Spain's 2023 animal rights law overhaul, meaning they are legally classified as tools with no protection — a welfare context that also signals they are commonly kept in harsh conditions and may be more fearful/feral when lost.
stance_target: Podenco baseline temperament when lost — trauma and fear response likely higher than pet-raised dogs
quoted_support: In 2023, there was an overhaul to the Spanish animal rights laws but devastatingly hunting dogs were excluded from these rights as they are viewed as tools and therefore not protected.

claim: Podencos have a profound awareness of their surroundings and sensitivity to their humans, making them likely to use sensory cues (voice, familiar scent) when near bonded owners even when in flight/stress mode.
stance_target: Familiar-person approach behavior in Podencos may be triggered by multi-sensory recognition even after flight
scope_conditions: Rescue/rehomed Podencos with established human bond
quoted_support: Smart, curious and clownish, they have a profound awareness of their surroundings and a sensitivity to their humans. Podencos reward their human companions with great loyalty, love, affection.
